wa_blood_sport.overview: A 50ft single-pitch sport/mixed route on Guye Peak in the Snoqualmie Pass area, combining bolt protection with one traditional gear placement; ends at a 2-bolt chain anchor with no top-out.
wa_blood_sport.beta: Steep start off a left-hand side-pull, placing a #1 Camalot into a pod before clipping into bolted protection; a rest ledge partway up breaks the pitch before a pumpy, sustained bolted sequence moving left and up to the anchor.

wa_first_amendment.overview: First Amendment (IV 5.11a, 9 pitches) climbs the northwest/west face of Le Petit Cheval at Washington Pass, established by Chris McNamara and Ian Nicholson in September 2009.
wa_first_amendment.beta: Opens with a 100-foot 5.4 chimney and hand crack, then follows hands-and-fists and finger-crack corners (5.8-5.9) through a choss-cave belay, before a 5.10+ series of corners and roofs taking thin protection (knifeblades/Lost Arrows useful); the 5.11a crux comes right off a belay in super-thin cracks, after which easier pitches join Spontaneity Arete's ridge to the summit. Route lacks a fully sustained or continuously natural line, with some dirty/vegetated cracks in places.

wa_northeast_face_direct.overview: Rarely-repeated alpine rock route on Mount Formidable's northeast face above the heavily crevassed Formidable Glacier in the North Cascades; first climbed in 2002, with a documented second ascent not until 2013.
wa_northeast_face_direct.beta: Lower pitches climb a rubble-covered class 4 gully into a snow couloir that steepens past 65 degrees with overhanging chalkstones near its top; the crux is two 5.6 pitches around the 'duolith,' a loose 30-foot granite tower split by a 3-foot crack, climbed on sparse protection (one 80-meter pitch protected by a single piece) before easier class 3-4 terrain leads up the north ridge crest to the summit.

wa_playing_not_spraying.overview: Playing Not Spraying (5.10, Grade III, 8 pitches, 800 ft) climbs the clean white granite south face of Silver Horn, a rarely-climbed sub-summit of Silver Star Mountain at Washington Pass.
wa_playing_not_spraying.beta: Opens with a layback off a large clean flake into a long left-facing corner system with finger cracks, crosses right below a roof into a splitter crack to a mid-route ledge, then a second roof leads to a hand crack and stemming corners with a hard exit, topping out via a deep summit chimney; protection is cams and nuts to 3 inches throughout.

wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.overview: East Face route on Poltergeist Pinnacle, the southernmost subsummit between Mount Challenger and Crooked Thumb in the Picket Range, approached via a glacier crossing to a dike-streaked granite shield. First climbed by Dan Aylward and Forrest Murphy, 2004.
wa_poltergeist_pinnacle.beta: P1's crux follows a dihedral into wild face climbing across parallel dikes to the shield's edge, unprotectable for roughly 40 feet before cracks appear halfway up (5.9, rated R for the runout). P2-P3 ease onto cleaner, better-protected corner systems with a small roof at mid-height; the upper ~800ft relents to low-fifth-class buttress-crest terrain, typically simul-climbed to the summit.

wa_south_gully_south_spur.overview: Alpine gully route up Guye Peak's south side: climbs a chockstone-choked gully, then exits onto the South Spur for the final push to the summit. Popular moderate objective near Snoqualmie Pass, climbed as a summer/fall scramble and, under snow, in winter.
wa_south_gully_south_spur.beta: Crux is negotiating one or two chockstones jammed in the gully -- parties are typically forced left onto the adjacent face (or stem/squeeze past on sometimes wet, loose rock) before regaining the gully and following the South Spur to the top. Protection is sparse and low-5th-class: a small rack of cams and nuts plus slung trees, with a hand line sometimes rigged over the exposed ground above the chockstones.
